title: Oil falls as ceasefire holds in the Middle East
section_path: Oil falls as ceasefire holds in the Middle East
section_path_text: Oil falls as ceasefire holds in the Middle East
summary:
Main
Energy markets pulled back as the ceasefire held in the Middle East. This stoked a risk-on tone which pushed precious and industrial metals higher. By Daniel Hynes
Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2026-05-06 121657.png | assets/2026-05-06_oil-falls-as-ceasefire-holds-in-the-middle-east_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_f8789ef91850.png

Market Commentary
Crude oil prices gave up some of this week's gains as the Middle East ceasefire held.

children.title: Main
children.summary: Energy markets pulled back as the ceasefire held in the Middle East. This stoked a risk-on tone which pushed precious and industrial metals higher.

children.title: Market Commentary
children.summary: Crude oil prices gave up some of this week's gains as the Middle East ceasefire held. The US downplayed the prospect of a return to active military action in the aftermath of Iran's recent attacks on the UAE, which included a strike on an oil terminal in the port of Fujairah.

children.title: Chart of the Day
children.summary: The massive disruption to Middle East oil supply has hit the jet fuel market hard. The region produces medium‑ and heavy‑sour crude - the barrels with the highest jet/diesel yield.
